text: India won the ICC Cricket World Cup in 2011, hosted jointly by India, Sri Lanka, and Bangladesh, defeating Sri Lanka in the final at Wankhede Stadium, Mumbai. MS Dhoni hit the winning six.
Sachin Tendulkar holds the record for most international runs in cricket history, and was the first player to score a double century in ODI cricket, achieved in 2010 against South Africa.
The Ashes is a Test cricket series played between England and Australia, contested since 1882. The trophy is a small terracotta urn.
Australia has won the most Cricket World Cup titles overall, with six titles (1987, 1999, 2003, 2007, 2015, 2023).
The Indian Premier League (IPL) was founded in 2008 by the BCCI as a Twenty20 franchise cricket league. Mumbai Indians and Chennai Super Kings are among the most successful franchises.
Lionel Messi has won the FIFA Ballon d'Or award a record eight times as of 2023, more than any other player in history.
Argentina won the FIFA World Cup 2022 held in Qatar, defeating France on penalties in the final after a 3-3 draw. Lionel Messi lifted the trophy.
The UEFA Champions League is Europe's premier club football competition. Real Madrid holds the record for most titles, having won it 15 times as of 2024.
Brazil has won the FIFA World Cup a record five times: 1958, 1962, 1970, 1994, and 2002.
Cristiano Ronaldo is the all-time top scorer in men's international football history, playing for Portugal.
Novak Djokovic holds the record for most Grand Slam men's singles titles in tennis history, surpassing 24 titles as of early 2024.
The Wimbledon Championships is the oldest tennis tournament in the world, first played in 1877, and is the only Grand Slam still played on grass courts.
Serena Williams won 23 Grand Slam singles titles in the Open Era, the most by any player, male or female, in that era.
The four Grand Slam tournaments in tennis are the Australian Open, French Open (Roland Garros), Wimbledon, and the US Open.
India won its first-ever Thomas Cup title in 2022, defeating 14-time champions Indonesia 3-0 in the final held in Bangkok, Thailand.
PV Sindhu is the first Indian woman to win an Olympic silver medal in badminton, achieved at the 2016 Rio Olympics, and later won bronze at Tokyo 2020.
The Badminton World Federation (BWF) conducts major tournaments including the All England Open, the oldest badminton tournament in the world, first held in 1899.
China has traditionally dominated the Thomas Cup (men's team) and Uber Cup (women's team) badminton competitions, winning the Thomas Cup a record number of times.
The Boston Celtics and Los Angeles Lakers are tied for the most NBA championships, with 17 titles each as of 2024, after the Celtics won in 2024.
Kareem Abdul-Jabbar was the NBA's all-time leading scorer for decades until LeBron James surpassed his record in February 2023.
The NBA was founded in 1946 as the Basketball Association of America (BAA) and adopted the name National Basketball Association in 1949.
Michael Jordan won six NBA championships with the Chicago Bulls in the 1990s and was named Finals MVP in all six of those title runs.
